rulings.Case Title: JIM HARMUTH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 9, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendants Cahill Construction Services, Inc. and Cahill Contractors, Inc.¿s joinder in Dillingham Construction, N.A., Inc.¿s motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.410 and 583.420(a)(1) and CRC 3.1342 is denied. Defendants failed to provide proper notice pursuant to CRC 3.1342(a) and plaintiff objected on those grounds. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAY WARREN CALHOUN et al VS. ASBESTO DEFENDANTS B*P AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Realign Defendant-Heir As Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 9, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs and Petitioners¿ motion to realign defendant-heir as plaintiff is granted. No opposition filed. Phillip Calhoun is realigned from the status of defendant to plaintiff. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: IRVING JUAREZ et al VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Realign Defendant-Heir As Plaintiff And To Correct Clerical Error In Plaintiffs Name; Pos
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 9, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs and Petitioners¿ motion to realign defendant-heir as plaintiff and to correct clerical error in plaintiff¿s name is granted. No opposition filed. Martin Silva is realigned from the status of defendant to plaintiff. Wherever the name ¿Koijoa Juarez¿ appears it should be substituted with plaintiff¿s correct name, Martin Silva. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
